title: Employing Tribe to Study RNA Interactions of Ataxin-2 in Drosophila S2 Cells
author: Shiva Kumar
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML

steps.title: Cell Lines
steps.instruction: Cell Lines: Drosophila S2R+ cells (DGRC, Indiana University)

steps.title: Growth media and supplements
steps.instruction: Growth media and supplements: a. Schneider's insect medium (Himedia, catalog number: IML003A) b. Fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Himedia, catalog number: RM

steps.title: c. Penicillin and streptomycin (Himedia, catalog number
steps.instruction: c. Penicillin and streptomycin (Himedia, catalog number: A001A) Reagents and kits

steps.title: Plasmids a
steps.instruction: Plasmids a. pJFRC7-20XUAS-IVS-8_ADARcd only construct [14] b. pJFRC7-20XUAS-IVS-8_Ataxin-2-ADARcd construct [14]

steps.title: Transfection a. TransIT-X2 (MirusBio, catalog number
steps.instruction: Transfection a. TransIT-X2 (MirusBio, catalog number: MIR

steps.title: RNA Extraction, library preparation, and sequencing a
steps.instruction: RNA Extraction, library preparation, and sequencing a. TRIzol reagent (Invitrogen, catalog number:

steps.title: b
steps.instruction: b. 1-Bromo-3-chloropropane (Thermo Fisher Scientific, catalog number: AC

steps.title: d. c. Isopropanol (Merck, catalog number
steps.instruction: d. c. Isopropanol (Merck, catalog number: I9516-500ml) e. Ethanol (Honeywell, catalog number: 32221-500ml) f. NEBNext poly(A) mRNA magnetic isolation module (New England Biolabs, catalog number: E7490L) g. NEBNext Ultra II Directional RNA Library Prep kit (New England Biolabs, catalog number: E7765L) h. Unique Dual Index UMI Adaptors RNA (New England Biolabs, catalog number: E7416L) i. NovaSeq 6000 SP Reagent kit v1.5 (Illumina Inc., catalog number: 20040719)

steps.title: Culture 1 × 10 6 S2R+...
steps.instruction: Culture 1 × 10 6 S2R+ cells/well in a 6-well plate overnight at 25 °C in 2 mL of Schneider's insect medium containing 10% FBS and 0.5% penicillin and streptomycin.

steps.title: Bring TransIT-X2 to room temperature and vortex gently
steps.instruction: Bring TransIT-X2 to room temperature and vortex gently.

steps.title: Meanwhile, dilute 2
steps.instruction: Meanwhile, dilute 2.5 μg of plasmid DNA in 250 μL of serum-free medium in a sterile tube and gently tap mix.

steps.title: Add 7
steps.instruction: Add 7.5 μL of TransIT-X2 to the diluted DNA mixture and gently tap the tube to mix the contents. Incubate at room temperature for 30 min.

steps.title: Add TransIT-X2 + DNA complex mixture...
steps.instruction: Add TransIT-X2 + DNA complex mixture dropwise to the wells containing growing S2R+ cells.

steps.title: Gently rock the culture plate to...
steps.instruction: Gently rock the culture plate to evenly distribute the complexes and incubate at 25 °C for 48 h. Note: To improve reproducibility among replicates, the constructs can be co-transfected with a GFP plasmid at a 9:1 ratio or expressed as RBP-ADARcd fused with GFP separated by T2A translation skip sequence. Combining this with FACS facilitates the selection of transfected cells, thereby improving the reproducibility and efficiency of this method. B. RNA extraction

steps.title: Collect 1 × 10 6 transfected...
steps.instruction: Collect 1 × 10 6 transfected S2R+ cells in a centrifuge tube and spin at 2,000× g for 10 min to pellet the cells. Add 1 mL of TRIzol reagent to the cell pellet and mix well by inverting the tube 5–6 times gently.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of 1-Bromo-3-chloropropane and...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of 1-Bromo-3-chloropropane and mix briefly for 10–15 s. Incubate for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 15 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 15 min at 4 °C. The mixture separates into a lower organic phase, an interphase, and a colorless upper aqueous phase.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the upper aqueous phase...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the upper aqueous phase to a new tube without disturbing the interphase.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL of 100% isopropanol to the aqueous phase and incubate for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 12,000× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 12,000× g for 10 min at 4 °C and discard the supernatant without disturbing the RNA pellet.

steps.title: Wash the RNA pellet with 1...
steps.instruction: Wash the RNA pellet with 1 mL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 10...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 10 min at 4 °C, remove ethanol, and air dry the RNA pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 20–50 μL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 20–50 μL of RNase-free water and quantify the RNA using a fluorometer (Qubit).

steps.title: Check the RNA quality using Agilent TapeStation
steps.instruction: Check the RNA quality using Agilent TapeStation. C. PolyA RNA enrichment and RNA fragmentation Note: The PolyA enrichment and RNA fragmentation were performed as described in the manufacturer’s protocol of NEBNext Ultra II Directional RNA Library Prep kit. The steps are described below :

steps.title: In a fresh tube, mix 20...
steps.instruction: In a fresh tube, mix 20 μL of NEBNext oligo d(T) 25 beads and 100 μL of RNA binding buffer (2×) and mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack at room temperature for 2 min or until the solution becomes clear. Discard the supernatant from the tube without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and add 100 μL of NEBNext RNA binding buffer (2×) per reaction to the beads and mix them to homogenize.

steps.title: Place the tubes on the magnetic rack and incubate
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on the magnetic rack and incubate. Once the solution becomes clear, discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of NEBNext RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of NEBNext RNA binding buffer (2×) to the beads and mix by pipetting until homogeneous.

steps.title: In another fresh tube, dilute 1...
steps.instruction: In another fresh tube, dilute 1 μg of total RNA with nuclease-free water to a final volume of 50 μL in a PCR tube.

steps.title: Add 50 μL beads (from step C
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL beads (from step C

steps.title: to the RNA sample tube
steps.instruction: to the RNA sample tube. Mix thoroughly by pipetting.

steps.title: Place the tube in a thermal...
steps.instruction: Place the tube in a thermal cycler and incubate at 65 °C for 5 min to denature the mRNA and facilitate its binding to the oligo d(T) 25 beads.

steps.title: Remove tubes from the thermal cycler...
steps.instruction: Remove tubes from the thermal cycler and incubate at room temperature for 5 min. Then, place it on the magnetic rack until the solution becomes clear.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads; do not remove the tubes from the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Wash the beads by gently adding...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads by gently adding 200 μL of NEBNext wash buffer to the tubes to remove the unbound RNA and mix by gentle pipetting.

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack at room temperature until the solution becomes clear. Discard the supernatant and remove the tube from the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Repeat steps C11–12 two times
steps.instruction: Repeat steps C11–12 two times.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of Tris buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of Tris buffer (provided in the kit) and gently mix.

steps.title: Place the tube on a thermal...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a thermal cycler set to 80 °C for 2 min and then cool to 25 °C.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of RNA binding...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of RNA binding buffer (2×) to the sample to allow the mRNA to re-bind to the beads, mix by pipetting, and incubate the tube at room temperature for 5 min.

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack until the solution becomes clear.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant while taking care...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant while taking care not to disturb the beads.

steps.title: Wash the beads by adding 200...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads by adding 200 μL of wash buffer (provided in the kit). Place the tube on the magnetic rack until the solution becomes clear and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Prepare a reaction mixture with 8...
steps.instruction: Prepare a reaction mixture with 8 μL of first-strand synthesis reaction buffer and 2 μL of random primer. Make up the volume to 20 μL with nuclease-free water in a fresh tube.

steps.title: To elute the mRNA from the...
steps.instruction: To elute the mRNA from the beads and fragment them, add 11.5 μL of the mix prepared in step C21 to the mRNA and pipette up and down 6 times to resuspend the beads.

steps.title: Incubate the sample in a thermal...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample in a thermal cycler at 94 °C and hold at 4 °C.

steps.title: Immediately transfer the tube to ice...
steps.instruction: Immediately transfer the tube to ice for 1 min as soon as it is cool enough to handle (~65 °C).

steps.title: Quickly spin down the tube in...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin down the tube in a microcentrifuge to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube and place it on the magnetic rack until the solution is clear.

steps.title: Collect the fragmented mRNA by transferring...
steps.instruction: Collect the fragmented mRNA by transferring 10 μL of the supernatant to a nuclease-free 0.2 mL PCR tube and place on ice. Proceed directly to cDNA first-strand synthesis. D. cDNA synthesis

steps.title: For cDNA first-strand synthesis, prepare the...
steps.instruction: For cDNA first-strand synthesis, prepare the reaction mixture on ice by adding 10 μL of fragmented and primed RNA mixture (from step C26), 8 μL of NEBNext strand specificity reagent, and 2 μL of NEBNext first-strand synthesis enzyme mix, and mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down at least 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate the sample in a thermal...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample in a thermal cycler with the following program: 25 °C for 10 min, 42 °C for 15 min, 70 °C for 15 min, and hold at 4 °C.

steps.title: For cDNA second-strand synthesis, prepare a...
steps.instruction: For cDNA second-strand synthesis, prepare a mixture of 20 μL of first-strand synthesis product (from step D2), 8 μL of NEBNext second-strand synthesis reaction buffer with dUTP mix, and 4 μL of NEBNext second-strand synthesis enzyme mix, and make up the volume to 80 μL with nuclease-free water. Mix well while on ice and incubate in a thermal cycler for 1 h at 16 °C.

steps.title: Purify the double-stranded cDNA by adding...
steps.instruction: Purify the double-stranded cDNA by adding and mixing 144 μL (1.8×) of resuspended SPRIselect beads to the second-strand synthesis reaction (~80 μL) (from step D3).

steps.title: Incubate for 5 min at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 min at room temperature and place the tube on a magnetic rack until the solution is clear. Discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Add freshly prepared 80% ethanol, incubate...
steps.instruction: Add freshly prepared 80% ethanol, incubate for 30 s, and then discard the supernatant. Repeat this step twice.

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack and air-dry the beads.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnet. Add 53 μL of 0.1× TE buffer and mix well. Incubate at room temperature for 2 min and place on a magnetic rack.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a clean PCR tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a clean PCR tube.

steps.title: For end prep of cDNA library,...
steps.instruction: For end prep of cDNA library, prepare a mixture of 50 μL of second-strand cDNA synthesis product (from step D9), 7 μL of NEBNext Ultra II end prep reaction buffer, and 3 μL of NEBNext Ultra II end prep enzyme mix, and mix them well.

steps.title: Incubate the mixture in a thermal...
steps.instruction: Incubate the mixture in a thermal cycler with the following program: 20 °C for 30 min, 65 °C for 30 min, and hold at 4 °C. E. Adaptor ligation using indexed-UMI adaptor

steps.title: Dilute the thawed NEBNext unique dual...
steps.instruction: Dilute the thawed NEBNext unique dual index UMI RNA adaptor on ice with ice-cold UMI adaptor dilution buffer ( Table 1 ) and briefly spin if needed. Table

steps.title: Recommended dilution of NEBNext unique dual index UMI RNA adaptor
steps.instruction: Recommended dilution of NEBNext unique dual index UMI RNA adaptor. Total RNA input Dilution requirement 1,000–250 ng No dilution 249–100 ng 10-fold dilution in UMI adaptor dilution buffer 99–10 ng 50-fold dilution in UMI adaptor dilution buffer Note: Keep the adapters on ice at all times.

steps.title: Prepare the ligation reaction mixture on...
steps.instruction: Prepare the ligation reaction mixture on ice with 60 μL of end prep DNA (from step D11), 5 μL of diluted adaptors, 1 μL of NEBNext ligation enhancer, and 30 μL of NEBNext Ultra II ligation master mix, and pipette mix. Incubate at 20 °C for 15 min in a thermal cycler.

steps.title: Add 3 μL of USER enzyme...
steps.instruction: Add 3 μL of USER enzyme to the ligation reaction mixture from (from step E

steps.title: and mix well by pipetting
steps.instruction: and mix well by pipetting. Incubate in a thermal cycler at 37 °C for 15 min.

steps.title: Proceed immediately with the purification of...
steps.instruction: Proceed immediately with the purification of the ligation reaction product by adding 70 μL (0.7×) of resuspended SPRIselect beads. Incubate for 10 min at room temperature after mixing well on a vortex.

steps.title: Put the tube on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Put the tube on the magnetic rack until the solution becomes clear and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash with freshly prepared 200 μL...
steps.instruction: Wash with freshly prepared 200 μL of 80% ethanol while on the magnetic rack. Repeat this step twice.

steps.title: Completely remove the ethanol and air-dry
steps.instruction: Completely remove the ethanol and air-dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and add 22 μL of 0.1× TE buffer to the beads. Mix well with pipetting.

steps.title: Incubate for 2 min at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 2 min at room temperature and put on the magnetic rack until the solution becomes clear.

steps.title: Transfer 20 μL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 μL of the supernatant to a clean PCR tube without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: For PCR enrichment of the adaptor-ligated...
steps.instruction: For PCR enrichment of the adaptor-ligated DNA, prepare the PCR reaction with 20 μL of UMI adaptor-ligated DNA (from step E10), 25 μL of NEBNext Ultra II Q5 master mix, and 5 μL of NEBNext primer mix. Mix well.

steps.title: Place the tube on a thermal...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a thermal cycler and run the program described in Table 2 . Table

steps.title: PCR program for adaptor-ligated DNA enrichment
steps.instruction: PCR program for adaptor-ligated DNA enrichment. Cycle step Temperature (°C) Time Cycles Initial denaturation 98 30 s 1 Denaturation 98 10 s 8–17 Annealing/extension 65 75 s Final extension 65 5 min 1 Hold 4 ∞

steps.title: For purification of the PCR product, add 45 μL (0
steps.instruction: For purification of the PCR product, add 45 μL (0.9×) of resuspended SPRIselect beads to the PCR product mixture and mix well by pipetting.

steps.title: Incubate for 5 min at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 min at room temperature and put the tube on a magnetic rack until the solution is clear.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Add 200 μL of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while on the magnetic rack. Incubate at room temperature for 30 s and then discard the supernatant. Repeat this step twice.

steps.title: Air-dry the beads on a magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Air-dry the beads on a magnetic rack.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and add 23 μL of 0.1× TE buffer to the beads. Mix well by pipetting.

steps.title: Quickly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin the tube in a centrifuge and incubate for 2 min at room temperature. Place the tube in the magnetic rack until the solution is clear.

steps.title: Transfer 20 μL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 μL of the supernatant to a clean PCR tube.

steps.title: Check the quality of the prepared...
steps.instruction: Check the quality of the prepared library on the Agilent TapeStation. F. Sequencing

steps.title: Load the PCR-enriched adaptor-ligated DNA to...
steps.instruction: Load the PCR-enriched adaptor-ligated DNA to the Illumina Sequencer platform NovaSeq 6000 paired-end flow cell and set the parameter to 2× 100 bp with dual indexing for generating paired-end strand-specific data.

steps.title: For sequencing reagent, use NovaSeq 6000...
steps.instruction: For sequencing reagent, use NovaSeq 6000 SP Reagent kit and run the setup, priming, and fluidics recommended by the manufacturer.

description: Validation of protocol Targets can be validated either through visualization of the gene edit tracks, as represented in Figure 2 , or by using Sanger sequencing. This protocol has been used and validated in [14] (Figure 3 and Figure supplements 4, 5, and 6). The cited figures represent the validation of a strong Ataxin-2 target, 14-3-3 epsilon, using Sanger sequencing and mapping the A-to-G modifications to the TRIBE edits. Data has also been made available publicly and can be accessed on Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO): GSE196739 . Figure 2. Representative edited gene track for Ataxin-2 targets. CG30158, one of the targets of Ataxin-2 identified by TRIBE analysis, shows editing events within its 3′ UTR (represented by blue bars) in the final edited gene track after removal of background and control-derived edits.
